summary: Spec and plan for adding lib.is_terminal_state(state) -> bool to .github/agent-factory/engine/lib.py. The function returns True for exactly 'done', 'failed', 'blocked' and False for any other value (including None and non-strings) without raising. Placed after has_fanout (~line 1388) to group all is_X state-query predicates together. Tests in a new focused file tests/test_is_terminal_state.py covering all 8 acceptance criteria.

ledger.why: lib.py is long (~1800 lines); the new function should sit near related state-query helpers (is_multiphase, has_fanout) for discoverability
ledger.what_i_did: Placed after has_fanout (line ~1387), before _render_leg_section. This keeps all is_X state-query predicates together without splitting any logical block

blast_radius.why: Pure addition, no surrounding code changes

reversibility.why: A function can be removed without side effects on any existing caller

ledger.revisit_if: The placement check fails or maintainers request a different location

ledger.what: 'blocked' is a terminal process state alongside 'done' and 'failed'
ledger.why: The issue explicitly lists it; verified it is used consistently in the engine as a terminal halt state
ledger.what_i_did: Verified: PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS lists 'blocked' as a terminal-label key (lib.py:803-808); next.py writes halted.reason='blocked' when an approval gate is rejected/overridden; not used as an intermediate state in any protocol fixture

blast_radius.why: Affects only the set literal inside the new function; existing code is unchanged

reversibility.why: The set literal in is_terminal_state can be updated if a new terminal state is added

ledger.revisit_if: The engine acquires a fourth terminal state; the function would need updating

ledger.what: New dedicated test file tests/test_is_terminal_state.py rather than appending to test_engine.py or test_decide.py
ledger.why: The issue asks for focused pytest unit tests; test_decide.py tests the decide() fold; test_engine.py covers path-CLI and CAS
ledger.what_i_did: Created tests/test_is_terminal_state.py with one test per acceptance criterion, mirroring the test-per-concern pattern used by test_correlation.py and test_decide.py

blast_radius.why: New file only; no existing test file is modified

reversibility.why: Can be merged into test_engine.py later without any functional change

ledger.revisit_if: Repo style guide requests consolidation of small-helper tests

ledger.what: No CLI subcommand needed for is_terminal_state
ledger.why: The issue makes no mention of a CLI subcommand; existing CLI-exposed functions are called from bash in GHA workflows; this predicate has no such use case today
ledger.what_i_did: Omit CLI registration. Verified no existing .yml file has a plausible call site for this predicate

blast_radius.why: Omission only; nothing is removed or changed

reversibility.why: Adding a CLI subcommand later is a small, isolated change in _cli()

ledger.revisit_if: A GHA workflow step needs to call is_terminal_state from bash
